message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Adding domain parameter support to trans tag (parse, _make_node, _install_callables)

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Adding trans_metadata support (note/created/modified) to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in ext.py

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Done: added domain= param to trans parse() (new domain var before variables loop, checked via 'if token.value == "domain"' with own duplicate/type validation), _make_node() now takes domain arg and emits dgettext/dngettext Call nodes instead of gettext/ngettext when set, and _install_callables/_install/_install_null now wire up dgettext/dngettext globals with fallback wrappers. All existing tests pass. Please rebase your trans_metadata parsing on top of this (domain block sits right after the token.value in variables check, before the assign/trimmed branches) rather than reverting it.

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Done: added note/created/modified metadata params to trans parse(), stored in separate metadata dict (not variables), validated as string literals, continue's early like trimmed. _make_node() takes new metadata param, sets output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty. My check sits right after the 'token.value in variables' duplicate check too, before assign/trimmed branches - same slot as your domain check but keyed on different token values (note/created/modified vs domain), so a merge is just two sequential if/continue blocks, no logical conflict. All 830 existing tests pass.
